_note: Oracle of Seasons (game 10, Game Boy Color 2001) static bundle fields. assemble-oos.mjs merges these with walkthrough.json (REGIONS) into app-data.json. Set in HOLODRUM: the General of Darkness, Onox, kidnaps Din the Oracle of Seasons and throws the seasons into chaos. Link wields the Rod of Seasons (change the season at special stumps — each season reshapes the land) and gathers the 8 ESSENCES OF NATURE from 8 dungeons to wake the Maku Tree's seed and defeat Onox. The under-realm of Subrosia and the magic-ring system are signatures. Pairs with Oracle of Ages via a linked password. Empty datasets degrade gracefully like the other classic games.

RUNES.name: Rod of Seasons
RUNES.from: Won from the four Season Spirits in the Temple of Seasons, down in Subrosia.
RUNES.what: Stand on a season stump and swing the Rod to change Holodrum's season — Spring sprouts flowers and vines, Summer dries water and grows climbing vines, Autumn raises mushrooms and drops leaves, Winter freezes water and piles snow into ramps.
RUNES.tip: The same screen is four different puzzles depending on the season. Stuck? Hop on the nearest stump and cycle seasons until a path opens.

RUNES.name: Wooden Sword
RUNES.from: Your first blade, found near Horon Village at the start.
RUNES.what: Basic sword for slashing and a charged Spin Attack. Upgrades to the Noble Sword and Master Sword later.
RUNES.tip: Hold the button to charge the Spin Attack; it hits all around you.

RUNES.name: Seed Satchel
RUNES.from: The Gnarled Root Dungeon (first dungeon) — and refilled from the Mystery/▼ seed trees.
RUNES.what: Holds the magic seeds you fire with the Slingshot or drop: Ember (fire), Scent (lure/damage), Pegasus (speed), Gale (warp), Mystery (random).
RUNES.tip: Different seeds solve different puzzles — Ember lights torches and burns bushes, Gale Seeds warp you to seed trees you've found.

RUNES.name: Power Bracelet
RUNES.what: Lets you lift and throw pots, bushes, and rocks blocking your way.
RUNES.tip: Many overworld rocks you couldn't move become liftable once you have this — backtrack for secrets.

RUNES.name: Roc's Feather
RUNES.what: Lets Link jump over pits, spikes, and gaps. Upgrades to Roc's Cape (double jump/glide) late in the game.
RUNES.tip: Jumping is core to nearly every later dungeon; pair it with Pegasus Seeds for longer leaps.

RUNES.name: Magnetic Gloves
RUNES.what: Attract or repel magnetic blocks and balls — pull yourself across gaps to magnetic anchors, or fling iron balls.
RUNES.tip: Switch polarity (N/S) to pull toward or push away; it solves the trickiest mid-late puzzles.

CHAMPIONS.name: Fertile Soil
CHAMPIONS.note: The first Essence of Nature, won from the Gnarled Root Dungeon (boss Aquamentus).

CHAMPIONS.name: Gift of Time
CHAMPIONS.note: The second Essence, won from Snake's Remains (boss Dodongo).

CHAMPIONS.name: Bright Sun
CHAMPIONS.note: The third Essence, won from Poison Moth's Lair (boss Mothula).

CHAMPIONS.name: Soothing Rain
CHAMPIONS.note: The fourth Essence, won from the Dancing Dragon Dungeon (boss Gohma).

CHAMPIONS.name: Nurturing Warmth
CHAMPIONS.note: The fifth Essence, won from Unicorn's Cave (boss Digdogger).

CHAMPIONS.name: Blowing Wind
CHAMPIONS.note: The sixth Essence, won from the Ancient Ruins (boss Manhandla).

CHAMPIONS.name: Seed of Life
CHAMPIONS.note: The seventh Essence, won from the Explorer's Crypt (boss Gleeok).

CHAMPIONS.name: Changing Seasons
CHAMPIONS.note: The eighth and last Essence, won from the Sword & Shield Maze — completing the Maku Seed.

ROADMAP.name: The Eight Essences of Nature
ROADMAP.note: One Essence waits in each of Holodrum's eight dungeons. Gather all eight and the Maku Tree forms the Maku Seed, your key to Onox's Castle.

ROADMAP.name: The Rod of Seasons
ROADMAP.note: Won from the four Season Spirits in Subrosia, the Rod lets you change the season at stumps — reshaping the land to open every path.

ROADMAP.name: Magic Rings
ROADMAP.note: Find ring boxes and Gasha-nut seeds, then have Vasu appraise them. Equip rings for powers — extra damage, defense, new abilities. There are 64 in all (shareable with a linked Oracle of Ages game).

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: Twelve are hidden across Holodrum — some only reachable in a particular season.

TIPS.name: Changing the seasons
items: The Rod of Seasons (from Subrosia) changes Holodrum's season when you stand on a stump and swing it. Each season transforms the SAME map: Spring blooms flowers/vines to climb, Summer dries up water and grows vine ladders, Autumn raises mushrooms and clears leaves, Winter freezes water solid and heaps snow into ramps.
If a screen looks impassable, find the nearest season stump and try all four seasons — one of them opens the way.
You start only able to set ONE season; you earn the others from the Season Spirits as you progress.

TIPS.name: Subrosia & seeds
items: Portals (glowing holes) drop you into Subrosia, the fiery under-realm — home of the Temple of Seasons, the smithy (sword/shield upgrades), and trading sidequests.

Magic Seeds power your Slingshot and tools: Ember (fire/torches), Scent (lure & damage), Pegasus (speed), Gale (warp to seed trees), Mystery (random). Refill from the seed trees dotted around Holodrum.
Gasha Seeds planted in soft soil grow Gasha Nuts that hold rings, rupees, and items.

TIPS.name: Rings & the linked game
items: Collect ring boxes and have Vasu (in Horon Village) appraise unidentified rings. Equip one at a time for effects — the Power Ring boosts attack, the Armor Ring defense, and dozens more.

Oracle of Seasons and Oracle of Ages are a linked pair: beat one, then enter its password into the other to play a harder 'linked' quest, share rings, and unlock the TRUE final boss (Twinrova → Ganon).
A bottled Fairy or a Gasha Nut potion can save you in a tough dungeon.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Pieces of Heart
COLLECTIBLES.note: Every 4 make a full Heart Container — some only reachable in a particular season.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Magic Rings
COLLECTIBLES.note: Appraised by Vasu; equip one for a power. 64 in all (shareable with a linked Oracle of Ages).
